armed-conflicts.headline: US-Israel-Iran conflict escalates, impacts global oil and stability.
armed-conflicts.summary: The conflict involving the United States and Israel against Iran has entered its third week, with Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei advising neighbors to shut down US military bases and maintaining a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Pope Leo XIV has renewed calls for an immediate ceasefire, lamenting "atrocious violence" and thousands of civilian casualties.

regions.name: Tehran, Iran
regions.detail: Iranian Supreme Leader made a public address, and the city was previously bombed in ongoing conflict.

regions.name: Strait of Hormuz
regions.detail: Remains effectively closed by Iran, impacting global oil supply and trade.

regions.name: Washington, D.C., USA
regions.detail: Democrats in Congress press for hearings and limits on the Iran war, and US actions are part of the conflict.

regions.name: Tel Aviv, Israel
regions.detail: Involved in the conflict with Iran, and US Embassy issued a security alert for citizens.

regions.name: Beirut, Lebanon
regions.detail: UN chief launched a humanitarian appeal amidst ongoing Israeli strikes and displaced populations.

regions.name: Dubai, UAE
regions.detail: UAE assures resilience amidst unprovoked Iranian aggression, and Iranian drones reportedly attacked an oil terminal in Fujairah.

regions.name: Naypyitaw, Myanmar
regions.detail: Myanmar's parliament meets with military retaining firm control after an election that excluded major opposition parties.

regions.name: Dniester River Basin, Moldova
regions.detail: Moldova declared an environmental alert due to a fuel spill from a Russian military strike on a Ukrainian hydroelectric plant.

human-rights.headline: Acid attack on Indonesian rights defender; deportees disappeared in El Salvador.
human-rights.summary: An acid attack on human rights defender Andrie Yunus in Jakarta, Indonesia, has been denounced as a cowardly act to silence government critics. Human Rights Watch reported that El Salvador is forcibly disappearing and arbitrarily detaining Salvadorans deported from the United States. Meanwhile, in the UK, human rights and health groups urge hospitals not to use Palantir software in NHS England due to grave human rights concerns, and an online webinar will outline these findings.

regions.name: Jakarta, Indonesia
regions.detail: Acid attack on human rights defender Andrie Yunus to silence government critics.

regions.name: San Salvador, El Salvador
regions.detail: Report of forcibly disappeared and arbitrarily detained deportees from the US.

regions.name: London, UK
regions.detail: Human rights and health groups raise concerns about Palantir software in NHS England.

regions.name: Vancouver, Canada
regions.detail: World Cup 2026 human rights plan is deemed weak by critics.

regions.name: New Delhi, India
regions.detail: Indian Supreme Court addressed judicial misconduct involving AI-generated citations.

regions.name: Quito, Ecuador
regions.detail: Constitutional Court ruled against automatic application of a rule requiring majority for gender modification on identity documents.

climate-events.headline: Major winter storm hits US, Canada; Western US faces early heatwave.
climate-events.summary: A large extratropical cyclone is producing a major blizzard across the Upper Midwest and High Plains of the US and parts of southern Canada, an ice storm in northern Michigan, and severe weather in the Lower Mississippi Valley and Eastern US, causing closures and power outages. Concurrently, the Western US is experiencing an unusually early heatwave with potential record-high temperatures.

regions.name: Illinois, USA
regions.detail: Blizzard warnings and related school/business closings across northern Illinois.

regions.name: Upper Midwest, USA
regions.detail: Affected by a major blizzard as part of a large storm complex.

regions.name: Northern Michigan, USA
regions.detail: Experiencing an ice storm due to the large extratropical cyclone.

regions.name: Carolinas, USA
regions.detail: Expected to experience severe weather and tornadoes.

regions.name: Southern California, USA
regions.detail: Facing an unusually early heatwave with record-high temperatures possible.

regions.name: Finger Lakes, New York, USA
regions.detail: Region impacted by warm, windy conditions leading to thunderstorms, then crashing temperatures and snow.

regions.name: Maui, Hawaii, USA
regions.detail: Flash flooding has been a major problem in recent days.

public-health.headline: Canada holds Day of Action for Public Health Care; Hawaii recall.
public-health.summary: Health coalitions across Canada are coordinating a National Day of Action for Public Health Care on March 16, 2026, to protest Alberta's Bill 11, which allows for out-of-pocket charges for medically necessary care. Meanwhile, the Hawai'i Department of Health alerted residents to a recall of Costco's Meatloaf due to potential Salmonella contamination, though no illnesses have been reported.

regions.name: Alberta, Canada
regions.detail: Controversy over Bill 11 leading to a two-tier health system and public protests.

regions.name: Ottawa, Canada
regions.detail: Federal government urged to enforce the Canada Health Act amidst provincial health policy changes.

regions.name: Honolulu, Hawaii, USA
regions.detail: Department of Health issued an alert for a Salmonella recall of Costco meatloaf.

regions.name: India
regions.detail: Celebrates National Vaccination Day to raise awareness about immunisation.

economic-crises.headline: Middle East war drives oil prices up, rattles global markets.
economic-crises.summary: Global crude oil prices soared as the US-Israel conflict with Iran continues into its third week, elevating risks to global energy infrastructure and causing the Strait of Hormuz to remain effectively closed. Central banks, including the US, Eurozone, and UK, are assessing the inflationary impact and geopolitical uncertainty, with expectations for some to hold rates despite prior cut anticipations.

regions.name: Strait of Hormuz
regions.detail: Its closure has directly caused oil price surges and disrupted global trade.

regions.name: Global
regions.detail: Oil prices have surged past $90 per barrel, impacting various economies worldwide due to Middle East tensions.

regions.name: Washington, D.C., USA
regions.detail: The US Federal Reserve is expected to hold rates amid inflation concerns from the Iran war.

regions.name: Eurozone
regions.detail: Policymakers are turning more hawkish due to inflation concerns exacerbated by energy prices.

regions.name: London, UK
regions.detail: Stubbornly above target inflation is causing concern over scope for more rate cuts.

regions.name: Tokyo, Japan
regions.detail: Uncertainty whether rates might need to be held steady due to potential economic damage from the Middle East war.

regions.name: Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia
regions.detail: Government allocates substantial subsidies to grapple with global energy price pressures.

democratic-erosion.headline: Myanmar military secures parliamentary control; US judicial AI concern.
democratic-erosion.summary: Myanmar opened its first parliamentary session in over five years with the ruling military and its allies controlling nearly 90% of seats, following an election that excluded major opposition parties. In the US, concerns about democratic norms arose as South Carolina considers bills to limit observing police and funds were threatened over perceived political insufficient mourning. Furthermore, the Indian Supreme Court intervened in a case where a judge used AI-generated non-existent citations, flagging it as a matter concerning the "integrity of adjudicatory process" and judicial misconduct.

regions.name: Naypyitaw, Myanmar
regions.detail: Military-controlled parliament convened after an election that did not include major opposition parties.

regions.name: Columbia, South Carolina, USA
regions.detail: State legislature considers bills to limit public observation of police and faced attempts to slash university funding based on political reasons.

regions.name: New Delhi, India
regions.detail: Supreme Court addressed judicial misconduct involving AI-generated citations, raising concerns about adjudicatory integrity.

regions.name: Jakarta, Indonesia
regions.detail: Constitutional Court revised an anti-corruption statute's definition to prevent over-criminalization and legal uncertainty.

humanitarian.headline: UN launches Lebanon aid appeal amidst Middle East conflict.
humanitarian.summary: The UN chief launched a flash humanitarian appeal of $308.3 million for Lebanon to support civilians affected by ongoing Israeli strikes, with 816,000 Lebanese internally displaced. Aid operations in Gaza continue to face restrictions, and Yemen faces significant humanitarian needs with 19.5 million people requiring assistance.

regions.name: Beirut, Lebanon
regions.detail: UN chief announced a flash humanitarian appeal due to displacement from conflict and ongoing Israeli strikes.

regions.name: Gaza, Palestine
regions.detail: Aid operations continue to face restrictions, and food assistance is at reduced rations.

regions.name: Sana'a, Yemen
regions.detail: Ongoing conflict means 19.5 million people, including 15 million women and children, need humanitarian aid.

regions.name: Dubai, UAE
regions.detail: Remains a major global hub for humanitarian logistics and continues supporting aid operations despite regional challenges.

regions.name: Tel Aviv, Israel
regions.detail: Ongoing strikes are causing displacement in Lebanon, necessitating humanitarian aid.
